---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
require(tidyverse)
require(tidytext)
load('~/Documents/work/data work/ned.RData')
```

```{r}
ned <- postDF %>%
  mutate(
    content=gsub("<.*?>", "",content),
    content=tolower(content), 
    content=gsub("[^0-9a-z \\.\\']", '', content),
    content=gsub('\\.', ' ', content)
  )
```

```{r}
head(ned$content)
```

```{r}
ned_analysis <- ned %>% 
  select(post_id, post_name, Date, content) %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, content) %>% 
  anti_join(stop_words) %>% 
  mutate(word=gsub("\\'",'',word))
```

```{r}
head(ned_analysis)
```

```{r}
# let's count the big words here
ned_analysis %>% 
  count(word, sort=T) %>% 
  head(20)
```

```{r}
extraStops <- tibble(word=c('people', 'time'))
ned_analysis <- ned_analysis %>% anti_join(extraStops)
```

```{r}
ned_tfidf <- ned_analysis %>%
  count(post_id, word, sort=T) %>%
  bind_tf_idf(word, post_id, n) %>% 
  arrange(post_id, -tf_idf) %>% 
  left_join(ned %>% distinct(post_id, post_name, Date), by='post_id')
  
```

```{r}
ned_tfidf %>% filter(n>=5) %>% head()
```

```{r}
ned_tfidf_matrix <- ned_tfidf %>% 
  filter(n>1) %>%
  select(post_id, post_name, Date, word, tf_idf) %>%
  spread(word, tf_idf) %>% 
  replace(., is.na(.), 0)
```

```{r}
ned_tfidf_matrix[1:5,2001:2020]
```

The Following code is taken from https://juliasilge.com/blog/sherlock-holmes-stm/ and uses topic modelling 

```{r}
require(quanteda)
ned_dfm <- ned_analysis %>% 
  count(post_id, word, sort=T) %>% 
  cast_dfm(post_id, word, n)
```

```{r}
require(stm)
topic_model <- stm(ned_dfm, K = 10, 
                   verbose = FALSE, init.type = "Spectral")
```

```{r}
ned_topics <- tidy(topic_model)
```

```{r}
head(ned_topics)
```

```{r}

ned_topics %>%
    group_by(topic) %>%
    top_n(10, beta) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(topic = paste0("Topic ", topic)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(term, beta, fill = as.factor(topic))) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ topic, scales = "free_y") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = NULL, y = expression(beta),
         title = "Highest word probabilities for each topic",
         subtitle = "Different words are associated with different topics")
```
That clearly didn't work. We need some other analysis to look for topics. Why not K-means on the TFIDF matrix? 

```{r}
names(ned_tfidf_matrix)[1:30]
```


```{r}
ned_clusters <- kmeans(ned_tfidf_matrix %>% select(-post_id, -post_name, -Date), 10, 20, 5)
```

```{r}
names(ned_clusters)
```

```{r}
blog_clusters <- ned_tfidf_matrix %>% 
  select(post_id, post_name, Date) %>% 
  mutate(Cluster=ned_clusters$cluster) %>% 
  left_join(
    postDF %>% select(post_id, title)
  )
```

```{r}
head(blog_clusters)
```

```{r}
blog_clusters %>% count(Cluster)
```

```{r}
blog_clusters %>% filter(Cluster!= 1 & Cluster != 4) %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% select(Cluster, title, Date) %>% arrange(Cluster)
```
This also seems absolutely useless! Absolutely no insights! 

Next, let's see if we can restrict to dictionary words (those for which part of speech is defined), and see it that can result in better results
```{r}
ned_english_tfidf <- ned_analysis %>% 
  inner_join(parts_of_speech, by='word') %>%
  count(post_id, word, sort=T) %>%
  filter(n>1) %>%
  bind_tf_idf(word, post_id, n) %>% 
  arrange(post_id, -tf_idf) %>% 
  left_join(ned %>% distinct(post_id, post_name, Date), by='post_id') %>%
  select(post_id, post_name, Date, word, tf_idf) %>%
  spread(word, tf_idf) %>% 
  replace(., is.na(.), 0)

```

```{r}
names(ned_english_tfidf)[1:50]
```

```{r}
ned_clusters <- kmeans(ned_english_tfidf %>% select(-post_id, -post_name, -Date), 10)
blog_clusters_english <- ned_english_tfidf %>% 
  select(post_id, post_name, Date) %>% 
  mutate(Cluster=ned_clusters$cluster) %>% 
  left_join(
    postDF %>% select(post_id, title)
  )
```

```{r}
blog_clusters_english %>% count(Cluster)
```

Again, KMeans doesn't seem to have any perceptible effect. 

```{r}
require(topicmodels)
```

```{r}

?LDA
```

```{r}
ned_tfidf
```

```{r}
nedlda <- ned_tfidf %>% 
  cast_dtm(post_id, word, n) %>%
  LDA(k=10)
nedldabeta <- tidy(nedlda, 'beta')
nedldagamma <- tidy(nedlda, 'gamma')
```

```{r}
head(nedldabeta)
```

```{r}
# How many documents belong to each of hte topics? 

nedldagamma %>%
  arrange(-gamma) %>% 
  distinct(document, .keep_all=T) %>% 
  count(topic)
```

```{r}
nedldabeta %>%
  group_by(topic) %>%
  top_n(10, beta) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(topic, -beta) %>%
  mutate(term = reorder(term, beta)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(term, beta, fill = factor(topic))) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ topic, scales = "free") +
  coord_flip()
```

```{r}
topicNames <- nedldabeta %>%
  group_by(topic) %>% 
  top_n(5, beta) %>% 
  arrange(-beta) %>% 
  summarise(topicName=paste(term, collapse='-'))

topicNames
```

```{r}
blogTopics <- nedldagamma %>%
  arrange(-gamma) %>% 
  distinct(document, .keep_all=T) %>% 
  left_join(topicNames, by='topic') %>%
  rename(post_id=document) %>%
  left_join(ned %>% select(post_id, post_name, Date, title, content), by='post_id') %>%
  arrange(Date)
blogTopics %>% head()
```

How have I posted on different topics over time? 

```{r}
blogTopics %>%
  group_by(topicName) %>% 
  mutate(postNum=rank(Date, ties.method='first'), prop=postNum/max(postNum)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=Date,y=prop)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~topicName) + geom_segment(aes(x=min(ned$Date), xend=max(ned$Date), y=0, yend=1), col='red')

```
The same thing, now by year 

```{r}
blogTopics %>%
  mutate(Year=as.numeric(format(Date, '%Y'))) %>%
  count(topicName, Year) %>%
  group_by(Year) %>% 
  mutate(Prop=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Year,y=n)) + geom_col() + facet_wrap(~topicName, scales='free')
```

```{r}
nedldagamma %>% head() 
```


```{r}

```
Very good. Fairly even distribution. Now to see what each topic has. 
```{r}
?get_sentiments
```


Then we'll look at the sentiment of the posts over time, to see how my mood has changed over the years
```{r}
head(sentiments)
get_sentiments('afinn')
get_sentiments('bing')
get_sentiments('nrc')
get_sentiments('loughran')
```

```{r}
ned_analysis %>%
  inner_join(get_sentiments('bing')) %>%
  mutate(score=ifelse(sentiment=='negative', -1, 1)) %>% 
  group_by(post_id, post_name, Date) %>%
  summarise(score=mean(score)) %>%
  mutate(Month=as.Date(paste(format(Date,'%Y-%m-'),'01',sep=''))) %>%
  group_by(Month) %>%
  summarise(score=mean(score), n=n()) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Month,y=score)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(span=0.1) + scale_x_date(date_breaks='years') + geom_vline(xintercept = c(as.Date('2007-04-11'),as.Date('2009-10-23'), as.Date('2012-06-16'), as.Date('2010-11-28'), as.Date('2016-09-15')), col='red', lty=2) + theme_bw()
```

```{r}
ned_analysis %>%
  inner_join(get_sentiments('nrc')) %>%
  count(post_id, post_name, Date, sentiment) %>%
  group_by(post_id, post_name, Date) %>%
  mutate(score=n/sum(n)) %>%
  mutate(Month=as.Date(paste(format(Date,'%Y-%m-'),'01',sep=''))) %>%
  group_by(Month, sentiment) %>%
  summarise(score=mean(score), n=n()) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Month,y=score)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(span=0.1) + scale_x_date(date_breaks='years') + geom_vline(xintercept = c(as.Date('2007-04-11'),as.Date('2009-10-23'), as.Date('2012-06-16'), as.Date('2010-11-28'), as.Date('2016-09-15')), col='red', lty=2) + theme_bw() + facet_wrap(~sentiment, scales='free_y')
```

```{r}
save.image('ned-analysis.RData')
```


```{r}
ned_phrases <- ned %>%
  select(post_id, post_name, Date, content) %>%
  unnest_tokens(phrases, content, token='ngrams', n=5, n_min=2)
```

```{r}
ned_phrases %>% head()
```

```{r}
ned_phrases <- ned_phrases %>%
  mutate(numWords=str_count(phrases, ' ') + 1)
```

```{r}
non_stop_phrases <- ned_phrases %>% 
  mutate(
    First=word(phrases, 1), 
    Last=word(phrases, -1)
  )
    FirstStop=First %in% stop_words$word, 
    LastStop=Last %in% stop_words$word,
    NonStop=!FirstStop & !LastStop
    ) %>%
  filter(NonStop) %>% 
  select(-First, -Last, -FirstStop, -LastStop, -NonStop)

````
```{r}
?word
```

```{r}
ned_phrase_count <- non_stop_phrases %>% ungroup() %>% count(phrases, sort=T)
```

```{r}
ned_phrase_count %>%  
  mutate(numWords=str_count(phrases, ' ') + 1) %>%
  filter(numWords==5) %>%
  head()
```



```{r}
non_stop_phrases %>% head()
```

```{r}
ned_stop_phrases %>% 
  filter(!Stop) %>% 
  group_by(Index) %>% 
  mutate(numNonStop=n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter()
```
